steps.title: Use the stated requirement
steps.content: The student wants evidence that Tamm’s journey was a true circumnavigation. The notes define one requirement for a true circumnavigation: the journey must begin and end in the same port.

steps.title: Remove weak support
steps.content: Choice A states the rule but then gives the trip’s duration, which does not prove that the route met the rule. Choice C asserts that the trip was a true circumnavigation but only mentions where it ended. Choice D mentions the three great capes, but the notes do not identify that as the requested requirement.

steps.title: Make the evidence prove the rule
steps.content: A useful sentence must do more than repeat that Tamm completed a true circumnavigation. It needs to show the specific fact from his journey that satisfies the stated same-port requirement.

steps.title: Show that Tamm met it
steps.content: The useful evidence must connect the requirement to Tamm’s actual route. Saying that he began and ended in Les Sables d’Olonne shows that his journey satisfied the same-port condition for a true circumnavigation. The answer is B.
